mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns because expanding factory output signals stronger corporate revenues and operating leverage over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes do not exhibit a positive Spearman correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy raises real discount rates and corporate debt-service burdens, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% episodes are followed by positive 60-day mean forward returns, or the negative return hit rate is below 55% over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between high consumer price inflation and a rising unemployment rate generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because stagflation simultaneously raises input costs and suppresses consumer demand, compressing profit margins and equity multiples.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and UNRATE does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (t-stat of interaction coefficient > -1.5) in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payroll growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during post-recession recoveries as labor market healing supports wage income, consumption forecasts, and reduces equity risk premia.
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery periods, PAYEMS acceleration fails to produce positive mean 60-day returns, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in real retail sales predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because near-term consumption weakness directly reduces revenue expectations and triggers rapid multiple compression.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly declines are not followed by negative mean 5-day returns, or the directional accuracy is below 52% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or greater rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as corporate earnings expectations reset lower.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases >0.3pp in 3m are followed by positive 20-day mean returns, or the negative hit rate is below 50% in a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy reflected in a high federal funds rate and negative real GDP growth produces strongly negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because the Fed cannot ease to support output, amplifying recession risk and corporate profit contraction.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high FEDFUNDS and negative GDPC1 growth fails to underperform the unconditional 60-day return baseline by more than 3 percentage points annually (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signals tightening credit conditions and weakening construction activity that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through reduced employment and materials demand.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1200 are followed by positive 20-day mean returns, or the negative hit rate is below 50% over a rolling 5-year window.
